title: erythroxylum williamsii
intro: Erythroxylum williamsii, also known as the Williams' coca or Williams' coca shrub, is a species of flowering plant in the family Erythroxylaceae. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of altitude sickness and as a local anesthetic, due to its high concentration of alkaloids, including cocaine and other tropane alkaloids. Erythroxylum williamsii is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fatigue and improving mental clarity, although its use is often limited by its potential for abuse and addiction. Herbal preparations of Erythroxylum williamsii are typically made from the leaves, which can be steeped in hot water to create a tea, or used in tinctures and infusions for medicinal purposes.
